focus: SKILL.md contract, reference route-map, validation/template fidelity, and relative-path claims

findingDetails.title: Reference mode names diverge from the authoritative workflow contract
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL.md defines Report-only audit, Structure validation, Content optimization, and Batch snapshot; workflows.md replaces them with Script-assisted review, Structure checks, Content optimization, and Audit snapshot.
findingDetails.recommendation: Rename reference table rows to the exact SKILL.md mode names or add an explicit alias map.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed SKILL.md, references/README.md, and references/workflows.md; mismatch appears at the route-map target for mode detail.

findingDetails.title: Worked example routes new skill creation through doc-quality despite the packet's no-creation boundary
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL.md says skip brand-new artifacts and never create a new artifact from this packet; workflow_examples.md starts with New SKILL Creation, mkdir, and writing initial SKILL.md.
findingDetails.recommendation: Remove/move the creation recipe or rewrite it as auditing an existing SKILL.md.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed primary WHEN NOT/NEVER sections and the worked-example reference; contradictory creation recipe is packet-local.

findingDetails.title: Changelog document fails the required validation goal under the requested reference gate
findingDetails.evidence: validate_document.py --type reference returned missing_required_section overview; template_rules reference requires overview and the changelog has no overview section.
findingDetails.recommendation: Validate changelog files with --type changelog or add a ## Overview section when using the requested reference gate.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Ran validate_document.py across every markdown file under the target; only changelog/v1.0.0.0.md returned a blocking issue.

findingDetails.title: Optimization reference claims SKILL.md lists impact/effort for patterns, but SKILL.md only lists names
findingDetails.evidence: optimization.md says SKILL.md lists all 16 by name and impact/effort; SKILL.md lists names only.
findingDetails.recommendation: Say SKILL.md lists pattern names and the pattern catalog provides impact/effort and before/after examples.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared optimization.md claim against cited SKILL.md section and transformation catalog.

noveltyJustification: First completed iteration; all four active findings are new and evidence-backed.
edgeCases: Changelog validation ambiguous because validate_document.py supports --type changelog, but dispatch requested only skill|readme|reference.
Stop policy requires max-iterations, but this LEAF execution completed one iteration and preserved next focus for dispatcher continuation.
ruledOut: No P0 route-integrity issue in primary SKILL.md.
No fabricated validate_document.py --type claim found in SKILL.md.

focus: command/path runnable examples and documented context for relative paths

findingDetails.title: Worked shell examples use a ../shared path from a directory where that path does not exist
findingDetails.evidence: The example changes into .opencode/skills/my-skill, then runs ../shared/scripts/quick_validate.py and ../shared/scripts/extract_structure.py. From that cwd, ../shared/scripts points to .opencode/skills/shared/scripts, while doc-quality documents the real shared backbone under sk-doc/shared from the doc-quality packet context.
findingDetails.recommendation: Rewrite shell examples to use paths that resolve from the documented context or add an explicit cwd preface to every copy-paste block.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Checked all command-example surfaces in workflow_examples.md and the packet README's shared-backbone context; invalid context-sensitive ../shared appears in worked shell recipes, while script interfaces themselves are real.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 correctness finding adds command-runnability evidence distinct from iteration 001's traceability findings; weighted new 5 over accumulated weighted total 21.
edgeCases: Runnable-example finding overlaps iteration 001's no-creation-boundary finding but is distinct because the path is wrong even if creation content were allowed.
Stop policy remains max-iterations; convergence is telemetry only before iteration 4.
ruledOut: No new P0 correctness issue: invalid examples block reliable use but do not directly cause destructive behavior or security exposure.
No fabricated quick_validate.py --json claim: the parser defines --json.

focus: reference overflow single-concern boundaries, duplication after dissection, and stale legacy content

findingDetails.title: Legacy Mode 1 taxonomy remains in overflow references after the packet was split into a nested workflow
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL.md and packet README define doc-quality as a nested workflow packet for existing-document audit/optimization, while workflows.md, validation_and_enforcement.md, and workflow_examples.md retain Mode 1/numbered-mode framing.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace legacy numbered-mode labels with current packet names and keep cross-packet pointers in Related resources rather than a numbered mode taxonomy.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Grep across target docs found Mode 1 in three reference files and numbered sibling-mode routing in workflows.md; primary SKILL.md and README use nested-workflow packet language instead.

noveltyJustification: One new P2 maintainability finding adds stale-architecture vocabulary evidence distinct from prior P1 mode table/path findings; weighted new 1 over accumulated weighted total 22.
edgeCases: P2 is separate from iteration 001's P1 mode-name conflict: it targets stale numbered architecture vocabulary, not the operational four-mode table.
Stop policy remains max-iterations; convergence telemetry is not used to stop before iteration 4.
ruledOut: No new P1 maintainability issue: primary SKILL.md still states the correct packet boundary.
No duplicate finding for existing no-creation and invalid ../shared shell-example issues.

focus: safety boundaries, edit permissions, destructive/overbroad operations, and fabricated-evidence safeguards
noveltyJustification: Terminal security iteration found no new active security findings; reviewed edit-scope, anti-fabrication, approval, placeholder-secret, and destructive-command surfaces.
edgeCases: Transformation-pattern auth examples use placeholder credentials but no real secrets were present; treated as clean for security.
Final review report generation was requested, but this LEAF agent can write only iteration artifacts, strategy edits, and JSONL appends; final report synthesis is blocked for this execution.
ruledOut: No P0/P1 security finding: reviewed guidance does not authorize destructive writes, credential handling, unsafe shell deletion, or unscoped edits.
No new finding for validator --fix: reference text limits it to safe issues and manual/approval context.
finalReportPath: not written: LEAF write contract permits only iteration artifact, strategy, and JSONL
